_meta.description: Compliance section reference input nodes. These 7 inputs are injected from the Reference model via the REF_OUTPUT_TO_TARGET_INPUT mapping pattern. After the Reference model computes, its output values are copied to these Target model inputs, enabling cross-model compliance ratio calculations.

instances.label: Reference HSPF
instances.note: Reference model's heating seasonal performance factor. Mapped from Reference output mechanical.heating.hspf.

instances.label: Reference AFUE
instances.note: Reference model's annual fuel utilization efficiency. Mapped from Reference output mechanical.heating.afue.

instances.label: Reference Cooling COP
instances.note: Reference model's cooling coefficient of performance. Mapped from Reference output mechanical.cooling.cop.

instances.label: Reference Cooling Intensity
instances.note: Reference model's cooling energy intensity. Used INVERTED in compliance ratio (ref/target instead of target/ref).

instances.label: Reference SRE
instances.note: Reference model's sensible recovery efficiency. Mapped from Reference output mechanical.ventilation.sre.

instances.label: Reference Ventilation Rate
instances.note: Reference model's ventilation rate. Mapped from Reference output mechanical.ventilation.rate.

instances.label: Reference Free Cooling Days
instances.note: Reference model's free cooling days. Used in pass/fail compliance check (target >= ref).

signature.inputs: These ARE the inputs (registered via graph.registerInput in ComplianceNodes.js). Populated by REF_OUTPUT_TO_TARGET_INPUT mapping.
signature.output: Values stored in Target model's MultiModelState, consumed by compliance ratio computations

invariants.condition: Values are always 0 until Reference model computes
invariants.description: Reference inputs start at default 0 and are populated after Reference model computation completes

behavior.condition: Reference model computes and REF_OUTPUT_TO_TARGET_INPUT copies values
behavior.output: Target model is recomputed with reference values, enabling compliance ratio calculation
behavior.note: This is the cross-model comparison pattern described in README.md

edgeCases.condition: Reference value is 0 (equipment not specified in reference)
edgeCases.rationale: Avoid division by zero; ratio of 1 means compliant when reference has no value
